content.full_text:
TO: CENTRAL FILE

THRU: J. PRITCHARD

FROM: Nan Hambleton

Code SS and Regulations referenced:

18215
18225
84305
86109 (f)

May 24, 1976

Mr. Ralph A. Heim
Regional Manager
Governmental Relations
Kimberly-Clark Corporation
925 L Street, Suite 290
Sacramento, California 95814

Dear Mr. Heim:

This letter is to confirm my advice to you on May 7, 1976, concerning a communication by Kimberly-Clark to its employees about Proposition 15.

You stated that Kimberly-Clark's Anderson mill plans to either mail its employees a letter in opposition to Proposition 15 enclosing a brochure furnished by the Shasta County No on 15 Committee or post such a letter on bulletin boards at the mill and make the brochures available. You asked whether Kimberly-Clark will be required to report on a campaign statement expenses of the Anderson mill in connection with the communication and whether the mill will be required to send a copy of the letter to the Commission.

I advised that the costs directly attributable to the communication will be expenditures which must be reported if they amount to $500 or more. Since you state that the expenses will be less than $500 and that Kimberly-Clark is not already a committee under the Political Reform Act, Kimberly-Clark has no campaign reporting responsibilities. If the letters are mailed, a copy should be sent to the Commission at the time of their mailing to the employees. If, however, the mill simply posts the letter on its bulletin boards, it does not have to send a copy to the Commission.

If the mailing is made at the direction or under the control of the No on 15 Committee, Kimberly-Clark will be making a non-monetary contribution to the committee which should be reported on its lobbyist employer report for the month in which the expenses are incurred.

Enclosed is a copy of 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18225 which discusses the expenditures includable in computing the cost of the communication, along with a copy of Section 18215 which defines non-monetary contributions.

If you have further questions, please contact me.

Sincerely,

NAN HAMBLETON
Special Compliance Representative

NH:mak
Enclosures

bcc: Jeanne-A
